title: The Prime Day TV Deals Worth Your Money
gist: WIRED TV deals guide for Prime Day covering premium TVs and streaming devices. Top deals: Hisense U7SG QLED at $850 (43% off from $1,500), Samsung Frame Pro at $1,498 (29% off), and Samsung S90F QD-OLED at $1,198 (29% off). Amazon launched its own 'Ember Artline' art TV competing directly with Samsung Frame—a notable Amazon hardware market entry.

themes.theme: TV Market Competitive Dynamics and Art TV Category

insights.claim: Amazon launched the Ember Artline art TV—available at $700 for the 55-inch during Prime Day—directly competing with Samsung's Frame Pro in the matte-display art television category, adding its own AI art generation via Alexa+.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: Amazon's entry into the art TV market with Alexa+-powered AI art generation represents a new competitive threat to Samsung's Frame franchise and a potential new Prime Day hardware halo product for Amazon's ecosystem.
insights.quote: You can generate AI artwork using Alexa+ or match art images to your room decor.

insights.claim: Hisense U7SG QLED sold at $850 (43% off from $1,500) was described as 'one of the best deals around this Prime Day'—a near-premium TV at a substantial markdown suggesting aggressive Hisense pricing strategy against Samsung and LG.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: Hisense achieving 43% markdown on a 2025 QLED TV at Prime Day indicates it is competing on price-to-spec ratio against premium brands—a continuation of Chinese OEM market share gains in North American TV market.
insights.quote: One of the best deals around this Prime Day is the Hisense U7 Mini-LED (2025)

insights.claim: The LG C5 OLED TV reached a new price low during Prime Day at $1,199 (14% off at Amazon, 52% off at Best Buy), having dropped steadily from its original $2,700 MSRP—described as 'the best TV for cinema lovers willing to spend.'
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: LG C5's steady price decline from $2,700 to $1,199 in a few months signals either OLED panel cost deflation or LG clearing channel inventory—relevant for LG Display and panel supply chain investors.
insights.quote: This TV has been steadily dropping from its original $2,700 MSRP, but this is the lowest price yet.
